components.name: Warehouse_shelf.cs
components.description: Representation of the data of each shelf in the warehouse and of the orders.

components.name: Warehouse_node.cs
components.description: Information of each node, its edges and its direction of circulation allowed in X and Z axis.

components.name: NodesRow.cs
components.description: Edges of nodes information, to automatically generate allowed connections between the warehouse nodes without need to specify them manually.

components.name: Warehouse.cs
components.description: Initialization of the robots and the nodes of the warehouse.

components.name: Warehouse_orders.cs
components.description: Assignment of tasks to each robot in a coordinated way.

components.name: OptimalRoute.cs
components.description: Backtracking algorithm for calculating the optimal path between nodes.

components.name: CameraControl.cs
components.description: Camera movement to allow the simulation viewer to move around the warehouse comfortably.

components.name: Warehouse_training.cs
components.description: Automatic training to modify the weights of the robot assignment formula to find optimal values that reduce total processing time between all robots.

components.name: Robot_wheels.cs
components.description: Control of wheels, servo-motors and its speed.

components.name: Warehouse_box.cs
components.description: Control of the gravity of the boxes to be able to pick up them.

components.name: Robot.cs
components.description: Robot control with kinematics functions, trajectory following systems, collision avoidance strategy, variables about the state of the robot and picking up and unloading of boxes procedures.

components.name: Robot_actuator.cs
components.description: Control of the pneumatic actuator of the robot.

components.name: Robot_arm.cs
components.description: Scalable script to control the joints of the robotic arm and its kinematics.

components.name: Robot_rightSensor.cs
components.description: Proximity sensor simulation capable of detecting objects in front of and to the right of the robot.

metadata.title: Software architecture of the Unity simulation

components.name: Web Interface
components.description: User interaction and speech input/output.

components.name: Unity Environment
components.description: Simulation environment.

components.name: Speech-to-Text Cloud Function
components.description: Converts speech input to text output.

components.name: CoppeliaSim Simulation
components.description: Simulation environment for robots.

components.name: Path Planning Module
components.description: Provides navigation instructions.

components.name: OCR Module
components.description: Processes image data to extract label information.

components.name: Robot Control Module
components.description: Executes robot commands.

comparison.explanation: The GroundTruth diagram focuses on the internal components of a warehouse simulation, detailing specific scripts and their roles in managing warehouse operations and robot control. It includes components like Warehouse_shelf.cs, Warehouse_node.cs, and Robot.cs, with connectors illustrating the flow of data and control between these components. The GeneratedImage diagram, on the other hand, presents a broader simulation architecture involving a web interface, Unity environment, and CoppeliaSim simulation. It emphasizes user interaction, speech processing, and robot control modules. There are no common components or connectors between the two diagrams, indicating they represent different architectural scopes. The GroundTruth diagram is more detailed in terms of specific software components, while the GeneratedImage diagram provides a higher-level view of system interaction. Rating: 5. The GeneratedImage diagram is accurate in representing a simulation architecture but lacks the detailed component-level information present in the GroundTruth diagram. The two diagrams serve different purposes and are not directly comparable in terms of content.
